PARAM.description: <code point> (the Unicode code point to escape if necessary) must be in the range 0=< cp <= Character#MAX_CODE_POINT
PARAM.name: cp
PARAM.content: <code point> (the Unicode code point to escape if necessary)

RETURN.name: the
RETURN.content: replacement characters, or {@code null} if no escaping was needed
RETURN.description: replacement characters, or {@code null} if no escaping was needed

PARAM.description: the sub-sequence must be a CharSequence
PARAM.name: csq
PARAM.content: Note: When implementing an escaper, it is a good idea to override this method for efficiency. The base class implementation determines successive Unicode code points and invokes escape(int) for each of them. If the semantics of your escaper are such that code points in the supplementary range are either all escaped or all unescaped, this method can be implemented more efficiently using CharSequence#charAt(int).

PARAM.description: the start index must be positive
PARAM.name: start
PARAM.content: Note: If your escaper does not escape characters in the supplementary range, you should either continue to validate the correctness of any surrogate characters encountered or provide a clear warning to users that your escaper does not validate its input.

PARAM.description: the end index must be positive
PARAM.name: end

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if the scanned sub-sequence of {@code csq} contains invalid surrogate pairs
THROWS.description: if the scanned sub-sequence of {@code csq} contains invalid surrogate pairs

PARAM.name: string
PARAM.content: the literal string to be escaped
PARAM.description: the literal string to be escaped

RETURN.description: The escaped form of the input string.
RETURN.content: The escaped form of the input string.

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if invalid surrogate characters are encountered
THROWS.description: if invalid surrogate characters are encountered

PARAM.name: index
PARAM.content: the index to start escaping from

RETURN.description: the escaped form of a given literal string, starting at the given index.
RETURN.content: the escaped form of a given literal string, starting at the given index.

THROWS.description: if invalid surrogate characters are encountered
THROWS.assertion: assert false; // No direct assertion for this condition
THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if invalid surrogate characters are encountered

PARAM.description: the input sequence to search for the next escape index must not be null
PARAM.name: seq
PARAM.content: the input sequence to search for the next escape index must not be null

PARAM.description: the start index of the search must be non-negative
PARAM.name: index
PARAM.content: the start index of the search must be non-negative

PARAM.description: the end index beyond which to stop searching must be greater than or equal to the start index plus one
PARAM.name: end
PARAM.content: the end index beyond which to stop searching must be greater than or equal to the start index plus one

RETURN.name: the
RETURN.content: Unicode code point for the given index or the negated value of the trailing high surrogate character at the end of the sequence
RETURN.description: Unicode code point for the given index or the negated value of the trailing high surrogate character at the end of the sequence
